text:
*[Bug 19029] New: Overly-long dlerror() may corrupt dyn.load() state*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19029>

            Bug ID: 19029
           Summary: Overly-long dlerror() may corrupt dyn.load() state
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>

Created attachment 3643
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3643&action=edit>
Suggested changes

Originally [discovered by Eric R.
Scott](<https://github.com/girke-lab/ChemmineOB/issues/48>). Mostly relevant on
macOS, but can be reproduced on Linux as well by overriding `dlerror()`:

```
#include <string.h>

char *dlerror(void) {
        static char error[1009]; // must exceed 1000
        memset(error, 'A', sizeof(error) - 1);
        error[sizeof(error)-1] = '\0';
        return error;
}
```

```
$ R_DEFAULT_PACKAGES=NULL LD_PRELOAD=./dlerror.so Rscript -e
'try(dyn.load("fail")); library(Matrix)'
Error in dyn.load("fail") :
  unable to load shared object '.../fail':
 

Loading required package: methods
Error: package or namespace load failed for 'methods' in dyn.load(file, DLLpath
= DLLpath, ...):
 SET_VECTOR_ELT() can only be applied to a 'list', not a 'NULL'
Error: package 'methods' could not be loaded
Execution halted
```

(Other failures can be induced by changing the size of the `error` string.)

With AddressSanitizer, the result is

```
==20288==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: global-buffer-overflow on address
0x55c236a19f08 at pc 0x7f8f55e602ca bp 0x7ffd5b9f4680 sp 0x7ffd5b9f3e30
WRITE of size 1009 at 0x55c236a19f08 thread T0
    #0 0x7f8f55e602c9 in __interceptor_strcpy
../../../../src/libsanitizer/asan/asan_interceptors.cpp:425
    #1 0x55c234e06209 in AddDLL ../../../src/main/Rdynload.c:877
    #2 0x55c234e12ebd in do_dynload ../../../src/main/Rdynload.c:1248
<...>
0x55c236a19f08 is located 56 bytes to the left of global variable
'Rf_osDynSymbol' defined in '../../../src/main/Rdynload.c:139:30'
(0x55c236a19f40) of size 64
0x55c236a19f08 is located 0 bytes to the right of global variable 'DLLerror'
defined in '../../../src/main/Rdynload.c:818:13' (0x55c236a19b20) of size 1000
```

The Unix implementation of `getError(char *buf, int len)` ignores the length of
the buffer and overwrites it using `strcpy()`, corrupting nearby variables in
the process. The Windows implementation ignores `len` as well, but I don't know
if the overflow is possible given the 4000-byte error buffer.

Patch tested on Linux using `LD_PRELOAD` and on Windows by artificially
shortening `DLLerrBUFSIZE` in `src/main/Rdynload.c`.

<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18984>

Kurt Hornik (<mailto:Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at|Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at
         Resolution|---                         |FIXED
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |CLOSED

--- Comment #1 from Kurt Hornik (<mailto:Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at|Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at>) ---
Part A (C level) was taken care of with c89311 in the trunk some time ago.
Part B (R level) will be left as is for now.
